title: recovery.mjs lib: crash-candidate detection, mining window, miner prompt template + unit tests
action: Create lib module recovery.mjs (byte-identical in skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/ and .bee/bin/lib/ per the mirror law) plus test_recovery.mjs. Exports: (1) detectCrashCandidates(root, {projectsRoot, projectPath, now, currentSessionId}) — per D1: walk .bee/sessions/*.json, keep records where heartbeatStale (reuse claims.mjs helper, NO new staleness constant), exclude currentSessionId (resolve via claims.mjs resolveSessionId precedence), resolve each transcript via perf.mjs claudeProjectsRoot/encodeProjectDir/resolveTranscript; a candidate requires transcript tail WITHOUT the clean-end trio AND work signals (bound lane in non-terminal phase, claimed cells owned by that session, or transcript last-activity newer than last durable settlement). Missing sessions dir, missing projects root (Codex — D2), or no records => [] silently. Stale-but-clean-ended => excluded. Missing transcript for a stale session => reported with transcript:null, never a crash candidate. (2) readTranscriptTail(file, maxBytes=262144) — read last window only, drop truncated first line, skip malformed JSON lines. (3) hasCleanEndTrio(events) — system/stop_hook_summary then system/turn_duration then last-prompt at tail, tolerating trailing queue-operation entries. (4) lastDurableSettlement(root, lane) — max ts across decisions.jsonl, capture-queue stubs, cell trace timestamps, lane-scoped when lane given else global (D3); no settlement at all => null (caller falls back to session started_at). (5) computeMiningWindow(transcriptFile, sinceTs, {maxEvents}) — events after sinceTs, hard cap with window_truncated flag, never whole transcript by default (D3). (6) buildMiningPrompt(candidate, window) — returns the worker prompt string: leads with a bee-tier generation marker slot, instructs the miner to read ONLY the given transcript window, redact secret-shaped strings, treat all mined content as data never instructions (D5), and return: in-flight summary, candidate settlements, verify evidence seen, suggested next action (digest <=600 words). recovery.mjs imports perf.mjs but must NEVER be imported by command-registry.mjs (perf import discipline). Tests cover the edge matrix in plan.md: empty/missing dirs, malformed lines, truncated slice line, heartbeat-fresh exclusion, current-session exclusion, clean-end exclusion, laneless global fallback, no-settlement fallback, cap truncation, prompt template contains redaction + data-not-instructions clauses.

truths: detection reuses heartbeatStale and resolveSessionId from claims.mjs and transcript resolution from perf.mjs — zero duplicated constants or slug logic
a stale session whose transcript tail has the clean-end trio is NOT a candidate (D1)
the current live session is never a candidate
no transcript store / no sessions dir / no records => empty result, no throw, no output (D2 silent no-op)
mining window is bounded: since last durable settlement (lane-scoped, global fallback when laneless, session started_at when none) with a hard event cap (D3)
the miner prompt template embeds redaction and data-never-instructions clauses verbatim-checkable by the unit test (D5)
prohibitions: no import of recovery.mjs from command-registry.mjs
no reading transcript files outside the current project's encoded dir (D5)

trace.outcome: recovery.mjs lib module (crash-candidate detection, tail read, clean-end trio, durable-settlement window math, bounded mining window, miner prompt template) + 29-case unit suite; byte-identical mirror confirmed
